title: CI/CD Workflow Health Audit
scope: All Receptor ecosystem repositories with GitHub Actions: supabase-receptor, planner-frontend, preference-frontend, workforce-frontend, match-backend, receptor-planner, website-frontend

findings.description: deno check supabase/functions/**/*.ts uses a glob that may not expand correctly on all self-hosted runner shells, potentially type-checking zero files and passing silently.

tasks.description: Replace the glob pattern in deno-check step with explicit enumeration (e.g. `find supabase/functions -name '*.ts' | xargs deno check`) or use a shell-safe glob expansion. Add a guard that fails if no files are found.

findings.description: smoke-test composite action DB canary (Check 4/5) queries /rest/v1/organisations but the canonical schema table name is orgs (confirmed). Every push to main fails the staging smoke test with a 404 after the migration was applied.

tasks.description: Update smoke-test/action.yml line 93: change the DB canary query from /rest/v1/organisations?select=id&limit=1 to /rest/v1/orgs?select=id&limit=1. Add a comment: '# table name confirmed as orgs per schema standard (260319 audit)'.

findings.description: prod-deploy.yml smoke-test-prod job calls .github/actions/smoke-test with only 2 inputs (base-url, slack-webhook-url) but the action requires 5 (base-url, anon-key, anon-jwt, incidents-webhook-url, deployments-webhook-url). Missing anon-key and anon-jwt cause PostgREST and DB canary checks to return 401, causing every production deploy to fail at the smoke test stage.

tasks.description: Update prod-deploy.yml smoke-test-prod job: add a Vault KV fetch step to retrieve anon-key and anon-jwt from secret/supabase/production/credentials, and incidents/deployments webhook URLs from secret/infrastructure/slack-incidents-webhook and secret/infrastructure/slack-deployments-webhook. Pass all 5 required inputs to the smoke-test composite action. Model the Vault OIDC auth on staging-smoke.yml pattern.

findings.description: prod-deploy.yml notify job reads secrets.SLACK_DEPLOYMENTS_WEBHOOK (no _URL suffix) via GitHub Actions secrets — bypassing Vault entirely. Canonical standard is to read from Vault path secret/infrastructure/slack-deployments-webhook for workflows with Vault OIDC access, and GitHub secret SLACK_DEPLOYMENTS_WEBHOOK_URL for runners without Vault access. prod-deploy.yml has Vault access and should use Vault.

tasks.description: Update prod-deploy.yml notify job: remove the secrets.SLACK_DEPLOYMENTS_WEBHOOK reference. Read the deployments webhook URL from Vault KV path secret/infrastructure/slack-deployments-webhook (field: url) in the same Vault fetch step as DR-03-T1. Expose as env var SLACK_DEPLOYMENTS_URL and use in the curl POST.

findings.description: prod-deploy.yml approve job depends on the GitHub 'production' environment for the manual approval gate. The 260312 audit formally deferred GitHub Environments. If the production environment has no required reviewers configured, every prod-deploy will auto-approve with no human gate — enabling unreviewed database migrations to production.

tasks.description: Configure the GitHub Actions 'production' environment in dm-ra-01/supabase-receptor Settings > Environments > production with at least one Required Reviewer (Ryan Ammendolea). Document this configuration in docs/infrastructure/ci-required-checks.md.
Verify and configure the 'production' environment in deploy-function.yml as well (it also uses 'environment: production' on the deploy job).

findings.description: deploy-function.yml uses actions/checkout@v4 and denoland/setup-deno@v2 (mutable tags) in both jobs. All other ecosystem workflows use SHA-pinned actions. Violates SEC-02.

tasks.description: Replace actions/checkout@v4 with actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4, and denoland/setup-deno@v2 with denoland/setup-deno@e95548e56dfa95d4e1a28d6f422fafe75c4c26fb # v2 in deploy-function.yml.

findings.description: deploy-function.yml has no workflow-level permissions block. The deno-check job runs on ubuntu-latest with default GITHUB_TOKEN permissions, which may include contents: write on some repo configurations. All other workflows declare permissions: contents: read (SEC-01).

tasks.description: Add 'permissions: contents: read' at the workflow level in deploy-function.yml. The deploy job requires id-token: write for any future Vault OIDC auth — add that per-job if needed.

findings.description: key-rotation-reminder.yml runs on a GitHub-hosted runner with no Vault access, so reading Slack webhook from a GitHub Actions secret is correct. However the secret name secrets.SLACK_DEPLOYMENTS_WEBHOOK_URL must be set (with _URL suffix) and documented as the canonical GitHub-secret name for runners without Vault access.

tasks.description: Ensure GitHub Actions secret SLACK_DEPLOYMENTS_WEBHOOK_URL is set in dm-ra-01/supabase-receptor repository secrets with the correct Slack incoming webhook URL for #deployments. Document the two-tier pattern in docs/infrastructure/slack-webhooks.md: (1) Vault path secret/infrastructure/slack-{channel}-webhook for Vault OIDC runners; (2) GitHub secret SLACK_{CHANNEL}_WEBHOOK_URL for platform runners.

findings.description: Unit test and build jobs in all three frontend ci.yml files still expose NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY as the stub env var. The LIFE-05 rename changed the application variable to NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY. Unit tests run against incorrect environment configuration, so any source file reading NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY receives undefined.

tasks.description: Update planner-frontend ci.yml unit-tests and build job env blocks: rename NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY stubs to NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY (matching the LIFE-05 application rename).
Same rename for preference-frontend ci.yml unit-tests and build jobs.
Same rename for workforce-frontend ci.yml unit-tests and build jobs.

findings.description: ci-cleanup job hardcodes NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: http://127.0.0.1:54321 and reads SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY from ${{ env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY }} which is empty at job start because GITHUB_ENV is not carried across job boundaries. The cleanup Supabase client authenticates with empty credentials — test org cleanup never runs and __ci_{run_id} organisations accumulate permanently.

tasks.description: Fix ci-cleanup in planner-frontend ci.yml: move the cleanup inline into the integration-tests job as an always() step at the end (after the 'Run integration tests' step). This avoids cross-job GITHUB_ENV propagation. The Supabase instance is still running at that point and keys are available in $GITHUB_ENV from the earlier 'Start Supabase' step.
Same fix for preference-frontend ci.yml ci-cleanup job.
Same fix for workforce-frontend ci.yml ci-cleanup job.

findings.description: All GitHub Actions in match-backend/deploy.yml and receptor-planner/deploy.yml use mutable version tags (@v4, @v3, @v5, @v1) rather than SHA pins. A compromised action tag could silently execute malicious code during image builds and GitOps manifest updates. Violates SEC-02.

tasks.description: For match-backend/deploy.yml: Pin actions/checkout, docker/login-action, docker/metadata-action, docker/build-push-action, and actions/create-github-app-token to their current commit SHAs. Use `gh api repos/docker/login-action/git/ref/heads/main` or equivalent to look up SHA for each action.
Same SHA-pin updates for receptor-planner/deploy.yml.

findings.description: deploy.yml build-and-deploy job has no timeout-minutes. A hung Docker build on ubuntu-latest will consume runner minutes indefinitely. Violates CICD-05 timeout pattern established in all CI workflows.

tasks.description: Add timeout-minutes: 20 to the build-and-deploy job in match-backend/deploy.yml and receptor-planner/deploy.yml.

findings.description: website-frontend has no .github/workflows directory. There is no CI pipeline: no lint, type-check, build gate, or deploy workflow. Broken commits can merge to main with no automated validation. This is an Active repository per the ecosystem map. Deployment mechanism: Cloudflare Tunnel routing to k3s prod (same pattern as backend services).

tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml for website-frontend with: lint job (npm run lint), type-check job (npx tsc --noEmit), build job (npm run build). Use SHA-pinned actions and arc-runner-website-frontend ARC runner label. Model on planner-frontend/ci.yml but omit Supabase, integration test, and codegen-check jobs (no Supabase dependency in website-frontend).

Create .github/workflows/deploy.yml for website-frontend. Deployment target is k3s prod via Cloudflare Tunnel (same pattern as match-backend/deploy.yml). On push to main, build Docker image, push to GHCR, update receptor-infra deployment manifest. Gate with GitHub 'production' environment approval.

findings.description: No repository has an automated rollback workflow. The rollback-runbook.md (LIFE-04) documents manual rollback only. ISO 27001 A.8.32 (Change management) requires recovery procedures — automated rollback enables faster MTTR and reduces human error in incidents.

tasks.description: Create rollback.yml for match-backend: a workflow_dispatch with input 'rollback-tag' that checks out receptor-infra, updates deployment.yaml image to the specified tag using the same sed pattern, and pushes. Gate with production environment approval.
Create rollback.yml for receptor-planner with same pattern.

Create rollback.yml for supabase-receptor DB: workflow_dispatch with target migration version input, runs supabase db push with a rollback flag or targets a specific migration tag. Gate with production environment approval.
Create rollback.yml for website-frontend with same GitOps pattern as match-backend/receptor-planner.

findings.description: Frontend repositories (planner-frontend, preference-frontend, workforce-frontend) have no deploy.yml. Deployment target is k3s prod via Cloudflare Tunnel (same pattern as backend services — build Docker image, push to GHCR, update receptor-infra GitOps manifest). Currently no workflow-as-code exists for environment promotion, approval gates, or rollback for these frontends.

tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/deploy.yml for planner-frontend. On push to main, build Docker image using the project Dockerfile, push to GHCR under ghcr.io/dm-ra-01/planner-frontend:sha, update the receptor-infra deployment manifest (same sed pattern as match-backend/deploy.yml). Gate with GitHub 'production' environment approval. Use SHA-pinned actions throughout.
Same deploy.yml for preference-frontend.
Same deploy.yml for workforce-frontend.

findings.description: The smoke-test composite action is called from both staging-smoke.yml and prod-deploy.yml. The incorrect table name (organisations vs orgs, confirmed as orgs) blocks both staging and production smoke test pipelines simultaneously. See DR-02 for the fix — this finding flags the blast radius.

tasks.description: Fix is the same as DR-02-T1. Confirm this single change unblocks both staging-smoke.yml and prod-deploy.yml smoke test paths.

findings.description: staging-smoke.yml reads Slack webhook URLs from wrong Vault paths: secret/infrastructure/slack-webhook-incidents and secret/infrastructure/slack-webhook-deployments (webhook after channel name). The canonical standard confirmed in 260319 audit is secret/infrastructure/slack-{channel}-webhook (webhook after channel). These vault kv get calls will return empty/error, so smoke test Slack notifications are silently broken and any vault kv get failure would abort the workflow before the smoke test even runs.

tasks.description: Update staging-smoke.yml lines 62-63: change path from secret/infrastructure/slack-webhook-incidents to secret/infrastructure/slack-incidents-webhook, and from secret/infrastructure/slack-webhook-deployments to secret/infrastructure/slack-deployments-webhook.

Delete orphaned Vault paths secret/infrastructure/slack-webhook-incidents and secret/infrastructure/slack-webhook-deployments using vault kv delete after the rename is confirmed. Document canonical Vault path convention: secret/infrastructure/slack-{channel}-webhook.

clarifications.item: DR-02/DR-16 — Table name in smoke-test DB canary
clarifications.decision: Resolved 2026-03-19 — canonical table name is orgs. Update smoke-test/action.yml to query /rest/v1/orgs.

Resolved 2026-03-19 — frontend apps deploy to k3s prod via Cloudflare Tunnel, same pattern as backend services (Docker image → GHCR → receptor-infra GitOps manifest). Cloudflare Pages is deferred minimum 6 months.

clarifications.item: DR-04/DR-08/DR-17 — Canonical Slack webhook Vault path and GitHub secret name
clarifications.decision: Resolved 2026-03-19 — canonical Vault path: secret/infrastructure/slack-{channel}-webhook (e.g. slack-incidents-webhook, slack-deployments-webhook). Canonical GitHub secret name for platform runners without Vault: SLACK_{CHANNEL}_WEBHOOK_URL. staging-smoke.yml was reading from wrong paths (slack-webhook-{channel}) — see DR-17.

implementationPhases.name: Critical CI/CD Failures
implementationPhases.rationale: Fix workflow defects that cause immediate failures: prod-deploy smoke-test missing inputs, wrong Vault paths in staging-smoke, wrong table name in smoke-test canary, and ci-cleanup broken across job boundaries. Restore all functional CI paths before security hardening.

implementationPhases.name: Security Gate — Production Approval, Website CI, and Frontend Deploy
implementationPhases.rationale: Configure GitHub Environment protection for production approval (prevents auto-approve on prod-deploy), create website-frontend CI and deploy pipeline (k8s/Cloudflare Tunnel), and create deploy.yml for all other frontend repos using the same k8s pattern.

implementationPhases.name: Secret Naming, Vault Hygiene, and Action SHA Pins
implementationPhases.rationale: Standardise Slack webhook secret references across workflows, delete orphaned Vault paths, document the two-tier pattern, and SHA-pin all unversioned actions in deploy.yml files to enforce SEC-02 supply-chain security.

implementationPhases.name: Environment Variable Consistency
implementationPhases.rationale: Apply the NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY rename to CI stubs to ensure unit tests run against the correct environment configuration matching production code.

implementationPhases.name: Process and Governance
implementationPhases.rationale: Implement automated rollback workflows for all repos (including the new website-frontend), and fix the Deno glob pattern to complete the CICD governance picture.
